free_text: Differential cross-section measurements for the
9Be(3He,3He0)9Be elastic scattering and the
9Be(3He,Px)11B reactions
4 MV Dynamitron Tandem Laboratory of
the Central Unit for Ion Beams and Radionuclides
of the Ruhr University Bochum in Germany.
The detection setup consisted of six silicon
surface barrier detectors of 1500 um in thickness.
The target consisted of two thin layers a 200 nm Be
layer and a 100 nm Cu substrate. A 3 nm ultra-thin Au
film was evaporated onto its surface for
normalization purposes and to ensure thermal stability.
the reported differential cross sections were
determined relatively to the cross sections of the
elastic scattering on Au or Cu.
The total uncertainty estimated from the
statistical uncertainty, resulting from the
determination of the peak area and systematic errors.
The systematic uncertainty consists of:
Errors in the determination of the Au/Be (3.6%) or
Cu/Be (3.4%) areal density ratios.
uncertainty due to the target homogeneity - 3%
Approved by V. Foteinou 25/08/2023
VS
Statistical uncertainty resulting from
the determination of the peak area.
